readout_roles_assumed_from_bridge_job.source: full_experiment=0 path has readout 1 mS=0 reference before Ramsey and readout 2 post-Ramsey signal.

literature_and_prior_context: Local literature index: Doherty et al., Physics Reports 528, 1-45 (2013), DOI 10.1016/j.physrep.2013.02.001, default NV-center reference.
Project note image231924_c01_ramsey_fft_expectation_20260512_0130.md computed f13C ~= 384 kHz from the weak-pi center using gamma_13C = 1070.5 Hz/G.

fft.method: linear-detrended fitted-reference-normalized readout2/readout1_line, Hann window; amplitudes are arbitrary and for bin comparison only

dominant_band.interpretation: broad carrier/detuning-like band; not a resolved symmetric 13C sideband pattern

fit_results.signal_for_fit: combined readout2 divided by a linear fit to readout1 reference

candidate_t2star_scale_seconds.recommended_model: Gaussian-envelope Ramsey decay on fitted-reference-normalized signal
candidate_t2star_scale_seconds.notes: Use the Gaussian-envelope value as a T2* scale, not sub-100-ns precision; exponential and stretched fits give the model-dependence range.

observations: terminal raw export contains 6 stored averages x 100000 repetitions = 600000 shots
bridge result completed normally; final post-run counts text was Final = 26.645 kcps
combined fitted-reference-normalized FFT has its two largest bins at 1.716 and 1.838 MHz; amplitude-weighted center of the pair is about 1.774 MHz
programmed carrier target bin ranks 5; expected lower/upper 13C target bins rank 3/19
per-average detrended normalized trace correlations are moderate and consistently positive: median 0.571, range 0.375..0.659
scan-order-aware drift diagnostic used Scan.ScanOrderEachAvg / mode snake and flagged averages []
raw readout2 and fitted-reference-normalized views agree on the main oscillatory band; point-wise normalization shifts relative bin weights but does not create a separate symmetric 13C sideband pair

the main oscillatory band is consistent with a residual electron Ramsey carrier/detuning component of roughly 1.76 MHz after the center correction; it is not a clean pair of sidebands separated by the expected ~0.384 MHz from a resolved carrier

claim_status.ramsey_signal: present: terminal raw readout2 and fitted-reference-normalized data show a repeatable oscillatory Ramsey component near 1.76 MHz across 6 stored averages
claim_status.t2star: supported_scale: Gaussian-envelope fit gives T2* ~= 4.0 us; exponential/stretched-envelope fits give a model-dependence range about 3.2-3.9 us. Report as about 4 us, not high-precision.
claim_status.carbon13: no_resolved_13c_coupling_supported: corrected-center terminal FFT does not show a clean carrier plus symmetric det +/- f13C sideband pattern. The lower target bin is present but embedded in the broad carrier/detuning-like band, while the upper target bin is weak; do not claim a nearby 13C from this dataset.

next_step: Project objective is satisfied for c01: aligned NV found, T2* scale supported, and no resolved 13C coupling is supported in the corrected-center Ramsey data. Further bridge work is optional only if operator wants a higher-precision T2* or a stronger null limit.
